name: BRUME D'OPIUM®
category: Home > TEA > Tea family > Blue tea > BRUME D'OPIUM
description: Plucked from a large, centuries-old wild tea tree in the Golden Triange. Dried leaves : The tender leaves and young, elegantly twisted buds decked out in majestic volcanic livery exhale a smooth perfume of frangipani flowers wet from the monsoon, joined by exquisite notes of pink galangal. Infused leaves : Luminous green streaked with garnet. Still warm, the leaves seduce with a gourmet note of praline followed by a fresh burst of rose water. Then from the leaves exudes a fruity-green score evoking freshly-picked starfruit. Liqueur : The opalescent orange liqueur assemblages into a round, cashew-tinted hue evoking elegantly fruity sensations of roasted apricot. A pleasantly sweet-and-floral nuance of dainty violet frames a delicious rush of candied quince. Authentic and unbelievably sophisticated.

name: HONYAMACHA-MEICHA
category: Home > TEA > Tea Origins > Japan > HONYAMACHA-MEICHA
description: « A green tea for eternity » To the west of Fuji Yama, the Abe and Warashina rivers crisscross the land alternating between galloping rapids and peaceful waves. They irrigate terroirs long celebrated for their unique grands crus, the famous « Meicha », elected by the Tokugawa Shogunate during the Edo period (16th – 18th centuries). Cultivated since the Kamakura era (12th century), today this fabulous green tea is produced exclusively for Mariage Frères while respecting the principles of organic agriculture and its natural environment. The small garden from where this tea comes benefits from a soil rich in minerals and a pure, invigorating mountain air. Long reserved for aristocrats and lords, this precious tea, « Emperor of the Sencha », has transcended time like an elixir of eternal youth.

name: JASMIN MANDARIN™
category: Home > TEA > Tea family > Green tea > JASMIN MANDARIN™
description: Plucked at dawn, thousands of delicate jasmine flowers are layered into the tea leaves and buds. The dry tea thus absorbs all of the heady, natural fragrance from the fresh flowers. This melding is repeated several times so the essence suffuses the heart of the tea. Between perfuming, the tea is dried again so that new, freshly plucked flowers may impart more aroma. After giving up all their delicious jasmine scent to the tea, the dried flowers are then carefully collected by hand, one by one. The all-natural jasmine tea is finally ready to be enjoyed.

name: MAORI BLUE SUPRÊME®
category: Home > TEA > Tea family > Blue tea > MAORI BLUE SUPRÊME
description: Dried leaves : The youngest and most beautiful leaves are harvested one by one, then carefully hand-worked to obtain a precious sheen of jade and indigo. The nose evokes fresh bamboo resin amidst an invigorating aroma of camphor wood. The heart of the leaves emit a note of patisserie recalling marshmallow. Infused leaves : Emerald-jade, the leaves open bountifully on a note of Bourbon vetiver and rosewood, carried by a hint of vanilla. The finale is mastered by a floral aroma of tuberose. Liqueur : Absinthe green. A fresh, downy veil of almond milk drapes over the palate. Frank and seductive, the finale balances between gelée de nèfle et une crème de figue aux noix. The ends of the earth in a cup of tea.

category: Home > TEA > Tea family > Black tea > MAORI NOIR
description: Dried leaves : The buds are harvested manually then delicately crafted so as to give a deep, matte ebony finish. The nose resonates with subtle, woody overtones. An elegantly floral aroma evokes the narcissus that grows along the volcanic foothills of the aptly named « Smoking Island ». Infused leaves : Dazzling shades of tan & amber. A fruity heart note gracefully defined by blackcurrant and plum leads into a soaring aroma of antique rose while in the background notes of cashew and autumn truffle linger about. Liqueur : Crystalline amber, fruity and supple like a quince poached in strawberry tree berry syrup, with an infinite persistence as invigorating as licorice candy. Black is très Beautiful!

name: ROYAL PURPLE TEA
category: Home > TEA > Tea family > ROYAL PURPLE TEA
description: Harvested in the sunny highlands of Kenya, ROYAL PURPLE TEA enriches Mariage Frères diverse colour palette of tea liqueurs with its beautiful purple hues. It is the result of a long process of selecting plants richest in anthocyanin, a substance naturally present in the leaves of Camelia Sinensis whose beneficial properties are innumerable; it gives the infusion beautiful, deep amethyst reflections. The liqueur is silky like rice milk, developing pleasant flavours of fresh walnut and fruity accents of white peach which linger delicately on the palate. Box of 30 round original French cotton muslin tea sachets, net weight 75g. The beautiful shades of purple may vary depending on the tea harvest and the brewing conditions, particularly the characteristics of the water. We recommend using water with a low mineral content. *Non-binding photo.

name: THÉ BLANC SACRÉ™
category: Home > TEA > Tea family > White tea > THÉ BLANC SACRÉ™
description: Women wearing fine gloves collect the sole pekoe (bud) of the imperial harvest with golden scissors, taking care not to harm the shoots. At no moment do the hands of the pickers — or of the planter — touch these leaves. The gloves prevent this risk, leaving the tea immaculate. Pure of any taint, this white tea is low in theine and naturally contains more antioxidants than any other green tea.

Its gentle infusion reveals a refined interplay of floral and fruity nuances, offering a truly sublime experience. Presented in a dazzling white canister adorned with a cascade of golden snowflakes, this tea is a masterpiece of elegance. The accompanying case, rendered partly in a tender green evoking the first buds breaking under the snow, is adorned with a striking crimson plum blossom, whose sensual beauty attracts joyous swallows. Together, they proclaim the arrival of brighter and more splendid days ahead.
